texto: Like Molière’s M. Jourdain, who spoke prose all his life without knowing it, mathematicians have been reasoning for at least two millennia without being aware of all the principles underlying what they were doing. The real nature of the tools of their craft has become evident only within recent times A renaissance of logical studies in modern times begins with the publication in 1847 of George Boole’s 'The Mathematical Analysis of Logic'.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ernest Nagel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Like Molière’s M. Jourdain, who spoke prose all his life without knowing it, mathematicians have …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, boole, george-boole pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de george-boole e jean-baptiste-poquelin.
